items.title: Feyenoord onthult: Sterling neemt rugnummer vertrokken Carranza over
items.summary: Raheem Sterling gaat bij Feyenoord voetballen met rugnummer 19. Dat heeft de Rotterdamse club vrijdagmiddag wereldkundig gemaakt.

items.title: VI Live: Bosz noemt rust voor Saibari 'noodzakelijk' en verheugt zich op Sterling
items.summary: PSV gaat vrijdagavond op bezoek bij FC Volendam. De Eindhovenaren willen de grote voorsprong weer wat uitbreiden met het oog op een extreem vroege landstitel. Volg de wedstrijd vanaf 20.00 uur op de voet in de VI Live.

items.title: Slot rueful as Liverpool injury bug strikes again
items.summary: Arne Slot has said the "last thing" Liverpool need this season is another injury after midfielder Wataru Endo was stretchered off against Sunderland.

items.title: Hull City v Chelsea: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup fourth-round updates, 7.45pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽ Live scoreboard | Follow us on Bluesky | Email John Last time out for Chelsea showed the slackness that has cost them this season. Will Unwin dug into Rosenior’s time at Hull. Rosenior was popular at Hull, appreciated for his hard work as a player and for bringing back hope as a coach after a downturn. Many think he was harshly treated when sacked by the owner, Acun Ilicali, less than two years ago but it inadvertently sent Rosenior upwards. Continue reading...

items.title: Pieters (37) vindt maar geen club: 'Helaas is de voetbalwereld veranderd'
items.summary: Erik Pieters is clubloos, maar de linksback zou graag weer aan de bak komen in het profvoetbal. Dat heeft de 37-jarige Pieters vrijdagmiddag verteld tegenover ESPN.

items.title: Opstelling PSV: Bosz kiest voor Flamingo, Schouten naar middenveld
items.summary: Ryan Flamingo keert vrijdagavond terug in de basiself van PSV. De 23-jarige verdediger vormt samen met Armando Obispo het hart van de defensie tegen FC Volendam. Hoofdtrainer Peter Bosz schuift aanvoerder Jerdy Schouten namelijk door naar het middenveld, terwijl Ismael Saibari rust krijgt.

items.title: Sources: Spurs finalizing Tudor as interim boss
items.summary: Tottenham are finalizing the appointment of Igor Tudor as interim head coach until the end of the season, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Sources: Chelsea's Macario closing on Wave move
items.summary: San Diego Wave FC are the front-runners to sign Chelsea forward Catarina Macario, multiple sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Madrid-linked Upamecano signs new Bayern deal
items.summary: France defender Dayot Upamecano has signed a new four-year deal to stay at Bayern Munich, ending speculation that he could join Real Madrid or Paris Saint-Germain.

items.title: Will Hearts topple the Old Firm and end long wait for Scottish league title? – video
items.summary: Ewan Murray discusses the reasons for Hearts' success this season and why Derek McInnes's side should be considered favourites as they hope to become the first team outside of Celtic and Rangers to win the Scottish top flight since Aberdeen in 1985 Continue reading...

items.title: When to watch Bahrain pre-season testing on Sky Sports
items.summary: Formula 1's preparations for its new era in 2026 come to a conclusion with one last test in Bahrain as the new racing campaign draws ever nearer.

items.title: Rodri mogelijk de klos na opmerkingen over arbitrage
items.summary: Manchester City-middenvelder Rodri is door de FA aangeklaagd wegens 'wangedrag' naar aanleiding van zijn uitlatingen over de arbitrage na het duel met Tottenham Hotspur eerder deze maand (2-2). De Spanjaard uitte na afloop zijn frustratie over een omstreden doelpunt van Dominic Solanke en suggereerde volgens de FA dat de scheidsrechter niet neutraal was.

items.title: Purpose and togetherness: What the soccer world can learn from FA Cup heroes Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield produced one of soccer's greatest Cinderella stories in beating Crystal Palace in the FA Cup, but the tale behind the triumph is even more remarkable.

items.title: Pereira signs deal as Forest boss until end of next season
items.summary: Vitor Pereira has signed a deal until the end of next season to become Nottingham Forest's fourth manager of the campaign.

items.title: Hull in title contention as Rhodes drops down to 13th after leading in Riyadh
items.summary: Charley Hull put herself in title contention at the PIF Saudi Ladies International in Riyadh but there was disappointment for Mimi Rhodes who dropped from leader to 13th.

items.title: Mercedes top last day of Bahrain test - but Russell warns of 'scary' rival
items.summary: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli and George Russell set the fastest times on the final day of the first Bahrain test ahead of Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton amid continued claims and counter claims from F1's four leading teams about who is really 2026's early leading force.

items.title: Blaming immigrants for problems is wrong, says Pep Guardiola after Ratcliffe comments
items.summary: ‘Society is better when we embrace other cultures’ Haaland doubt for FA Cup tie, Rodri charged by FA Pep Guardiola has said that blaming people from overseas for a country’s problems is wrong, the Manchester City manager’s comments coming amid the fallout of Sir Jim Ratcliffe claiming the UK is being “colonised by immigrants”. Ratcliffe’s comment, made in an interview with Sky News on Wednesday, has led to widespread condemnation, including from within football, leading to Manchester United’s single largest minority owner saying he was sorry that his “choice of language has offended some people in the UK and Europe”. Continue reading...

items.title: Meet the 'firefighter' Croatian tasked with keeping Spurs in top flight
items.summary: Igor Tudor is tasked with keeping Tottenham in the Premier League - his toughest firefighting mission yet.

items.title: Spurs agree deal to make Igor Tudor interim manager until end of season
items.summary: Tudor’s former clubs include Juventus and Marseille Door remains open for summer Pochettino return Tottenham have reached an agreement for Igor Tudor to become their interim manager until the end of the season, leaving the door open for Mauricio Pochettino to return this summer. The club turned to Tudor after making checks on the former Borussia Dortmund manager Edin Terzic and the former RB Leizpig manager Marco Rose. The former Croatia international has been out of work since he left Juventus in October after the Serie A club went eight games without a win. Continue reading...

items.title: Man City's Rodri charged over 'not fair' referee comments
items.summary: Manchester City midfielder Rodri is charged with misconduct by the Football Association following comments he made about referees following his side's 2-2 draw with Tottenham earlier this month.
